architecture_pattern: Layered Architecture (Presentation/API Layer, Business Logic Layer, Data Access Layer)
summary: A secure, scalable backend-only REST API for task management built with FastAPI. Implements JWT-based authentication and authorization, supports CRUD operations on tasks scoped to authenticated users, and follows separation of concerns with clear layers.

components.name: Authentication Service
components.responsibility: Handles user registration, login, JWT token generation, validation, and password hashing.

components.name: Task Service
components.responsibility: Manages business logic for task creation, retrieval, updating, and deletion with ownership enforcement.

components.name: User Repository
components.responsibility: Performs CRUD operations on User entities in the database.

components.name: Task Repository
components.responsibility: Performs CRUD operations on Task entities with user-scoped queries.

components.name: Database Adapter
components.responsibility: Manages database connection pooling, migrations, and session lifecycle.

api_endpoints.description: Register a new user with username and password.
Authenticate user and return JWT access token.
Retrieve all tasks owned by the authenticated user.
Retrieve a specific task owned by the authenticated user.
Create a new task for the authenticated user.
Update an existing task owned by the authenticated user.
Delete a task owned by the authenticated user.

columns.name: user_id
constraints: FOREIGN KEY references users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE

tech_stack.cache: Redis (optional, for future token revocation or rate limiting)

deployment_strategy: Containerized deployment via Docker; orchestrated using Kubernetes or Docker Compose for staging/production. CI/CD via GitHub Actions with automated testing, linting, and image build/push. Database migrations managed via Alembic.
security_considerations: Password hashing using bcrypt via passlib
JWT tokens signed with HS256 and stored securely (HTTP-only cookies or bearer auth with short expiry)
All endpoints except /auth/register and /auth/login require valid JWT
SQL injection prevention via parameterized queries (SQLAlchemy ORM)
Rate limiting on auth endpoints (to be implemented via middleware)
scalability_notes: Database read replicas can be added for /tasks GET scalability
Connection pooling configured via SQLAlchemy and asyncpg
Caching layer (Redis) can be introduced for frequent task list queries
Task ownership scoping ensures data isolation and simplifies sharding strategies
